stem: An engineer takes 20 measurements of vehicle speed using a speedometer. The measurements have a mean of 152.5 km/h and a standard deviation of 15.4 km/h. However, calibration tests reveal that a speedometer systematically underestimates the true value by between 2.4% and 6.0%. Estimate the true vehicle speed and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 152.5 km/h. After correcting for the 4.2% bias: ≈ 159.2 km/h. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (2.4%-6.0%) and sampling variability: [149.3, 169.1]. Point estimate: 159.2 km/h.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
